history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right. The cell directly ahead at row 02 column C is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. I need to avoid the lava column and head downward along the safe path on the left side. To do that from facing right, I should turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing down. The path below me in column B is clear, while the right side is blocked by lava at C. Since the goal is lower on the map, the safest move is to continue downward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column B facing down. The cell directly ahead at row 04 column B is open, and continuing downward keeps me on the safe path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B facing down. The next cell in front of me is row 05 column B, which is safe, so I should keep moving down the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 05 column B facing down. The cell ahead at row 06 column B is open, and moving forward continues safely toward the lower corridor that leads to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 06 column B facing down. The cell ahead at row 07 column B is safe floor, so I should continue forward rather than turning.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column B facing down. The cell directly ahead at row 08 column B is open, but the goal is to the right on row 08 column H, so I need to move down into the open corridor first.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am now at row 08 column B facing down. The goal is to the right on the same row, so I need to turn right to face toward the goal corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing left (`<`). The goal is to my right along the same row, so I need to turn around by turning left once to face down? Actually from left, turning right would face up and turning left would face down, neither is toward the goal. The safe way is to turn right twice, but only one action is allowed now, so I should start by turning right to reorient toward the corridor and continue turning next.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing up (`^`). The goal is to the right on the same row, and the cells to the right are safe floor cells leading to G. To face right, I need to turn clockwise.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is several cells to the right on the same row, and the path along row 08 is clear floor cells until G. The next safe step is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am now at row 08 column C facing right. The goal is still to the right, and the next cell row 08 column D is safe floor. I should continue forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column D facing right. The goal is still to the right, and the next cell row 08 column E is safe floor. I should keep moving forward toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column E facing right. The goal is at row 08 column H, and the cells ahead at F and G appear safe floor. I should move forward toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column F facing right. The goal is to my right at row 08 column H, and the next cell row 08 column G is safe floor. I should keep moving forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column G facing right, and the goal is immediately ahead at row 08 column H. The next cell is the goal, so I should move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
